title: Alkermes — Psychiatry — Knowledge Doc
html:
Market Context: Alkermes Psychiatry Portfolio (Lybalvi, Aristada, Vivitrol)

Client: Alkermes, Inc. (mid-size specialty pharma, ~$1.5B total revenue FY2025) Portfolio: LYBALVI (olanzapine/samidorphan), ARISTADA (aripiprazole LAI), VIVITROL (naltrexone ER injection) Indications: Schizophrenia, Bipolar I Disorder, Alcohol Use Disorder, Opioid Use Disorder Document scope: Multi-brand portfolio-level competitive/clinical landscape Last updated: April 2026

Governing Thought

Cobenfy's September 2024 approval as the first new-mechanism schizophrenia drug in 35 years has restructured the competitive landscape, but its clinical stumbles (ARISE adjunctive trial failure, modest efficacy narrative) and slow early uptake ($155M in year-one sales) give Alkermes a window to entrench Lybalvi's position as the best risk-managed oral antipsychotic and Aristada's position as the adherence-optimized LAI. The strategic question is not whether Cobenfy threatens Alkermes — it does, eventually — but whether Alkermes can convert its weight-mitigation story and LAI convenience story into durable market positions before Cobenfy's real-world data matures and potential label expansions (bipolar, Alzheimer's psychosis) widen its addressable market.

1. Disease Landscape

1.1 Schizophrenia

Epidemiology: ~3.3 million adults in the US live with schizophrenia. Approximately 200,000-250,000 new diagnoses annually. Lifetime prevalence ~1% globally. Onset typically ages 16-30.

Treatment paradigm: Antipsychotics remain the backbone. The field has operated on D2 receptor blockade for 70 years — first-generation (typicals) and second-generation (atypicals) both work primarily through dopamine antagonism. Side effects — particularly metabolic syndrome, weight gain, EPS, tardive dyskinesia — drive treatment selection more than efficacy differences between agents.

What changed in 2024: Cobenfy (xanomeline/trospium, BMS) was approved September 2024 as the first muscarinic agonist for schizophrenia. It does NOT block D2 receptors. This is the most significant mechanism innovation since clozapine (1990). The implications are not yet fully understood, but the conceptual shift is enormous: schizophrenia treatment no longer requires dopamine blockade.

Key unmet needs:

  1. Metabolic safety: Weight gain remains the #1 reason for non-adherence with atypical antipsychotics. Olanzapine is among the most efficacious but also the most metabolically toxic — Lybalvi was designed to address exactly this gap.
  2. Adherence: ~50% of schizophrenia patients are partially or fully non-adherent within 12 months. LAIs (Aristada, Invega Sustenna/Trinza/Hafyera, Abilify Maintena, Uzedy) exist to solve this but are underutilized (~30% of eligible patients receive LAIs).
  3. Negative symptoms and cognition: Current antipsychotics primarily address positive symptoms. Cobenfy's muscarinic mechanism shows early signals on cognitive improvement — if validated, this becomes a differentiation axis no existing drug can match.
  4. Treatment-resistant schizophrenia: Clozapine remains the only approved option; it is severely underused due to REMS monitoring burden.

1.2 Bipolar I Disorder

Epidemiology: ~6 million US adults with bipolar disorder; ~2.8% lifetime prevalence. Bipolar I (manic episodes) is the primary Lybalvi indication.

Treatment paradigm: Mood stabilizers (lithium, valproate) plus atypical antipsychotics for acute mania and maintenance. Olanzapine is highly effective for acute mania but weight/metabolic burden limits long-term use — Lybalvi's mitigation story is equally relevant here.

Competitive set for Lybalvi in BPD: Vraylar (cariprazine), Caplyta (lumateperone), Rexulti (brexpiprazole), Seroquel/generic quetiapine. The 2025 LYBALVI PET SOW explicitly tracks Vraylar, Caplyta, and Rexulti as key competitors.

1.3 Addiction Medicine (Vivitrol)

Opioid Use Disorder (OUD): ~5.6 million US adults with OUD. The opioid crisis has driven Medicaid expansion, criminal justice mandates, and increased MAT (medication-assisted treatment) utilization. Market growing at 11% CAGR to projected $10.2B by 2030.

Alcohol Use Disorder (AUD): ~29.5 million US adults. Vastly undertreated — only ~7% receive any medication. Vivitrol's AUD indication is driving growth.

Key competitive dynamics:

  • Buprenorphine segment holds 73.5% of the OUD medication market (Sublocade, Brixadi, Suboxone/generics)
  • Vivitrol competes as the only non-opioid injection option (naltrexone = opioid antagonist, not partial agonist)
  • Sublocade (buprenorphine SC, Indivior) received updated label in Feb 2025 with 12-month retention data — directly competitive
  • Vivitrol's differentiated position: criminal justice system preference (no diversion risk), AUD dual indication, monthly injection convenience

2. Product Profiles

2.1 LYBALVI (olanzapine + samidorphan) — THE GROWTH DRIVER

Mechanism: Combines olanzapine (the most efficacious atypical antipsychotic by many measures) with samidorphan (opioid receptor antagonist) to mitigate olanzapine-associated weight gain. Samidorphan modulates the endogenous opioid system's role in weight/metabolic regulation.

Indications: Schizophrenia in adults; Bipolar I Disorder (acute manic/mixed episodes, maintenance monotherapy, adjunct to lithium/valproate) in adults.

The pitch: "Olanzapine's efficacy without the weight gain problem."

Key efficacy data:

  • ENLIGHTEN-2 (Phase 3): At 24 weeks, mean percent weight change: +4.21% (Lybalvi) vs +6.59% (olanzapine alone). Significantly fewer patients with >=10% weight gain (17.8% vs 29.8%) and >=7% weight gain (27.5% vs 42.7%)
  • 4-year real-world data (2024-2025): Mean body weight change of just +1.47 kg over 4 years. Stable lipid and glycemic parameters. This is the strongest long-term tolerability evidence in the class.
  • Meta-analysis (Jan 2025): Individual patient data meta-analysis across Phase 2 and 3 studies confirmed consistent weight mitigation across subgroups

Revenue trajectory:

Year Lybalvi Net Sales YoY Growth
2023 ~$210M (est.) —
2024 $280.0M ~33%
2025 $346.7M 24%
2026E $380-400M 10-15%

TRx growth: 24% in FY2025, driven by new patient starts and expanded prescriber breadth.

ZoomRx engagement: 2025 LYBALVI Multichannel Promotional Effectiveness Tracking ($338,701 SOW, 2 full + 2 partial waves). Tracks SoV vs Vraylar, Caplyta, Rexulti. Notably, Cobenfy reach/frequency/SoV is being tracked along with sample-related questions — Alkermes is watching Cobenfy closely. Also proposed: Sales Force Tactical Optimization ($89,500) to optimize detail delivery for SZ and BPD prescribing.

2.2 ARISTADA (aripiprazole lauroxil) — MATURE LAI FRANCHISE

Mechanism: Long-acting injectable aripiprazole formulation. Partial D2 agonist (distinct from full D2 antagonists). Available in monthly, 6-week, and 2-month formulations. ARISTADA INITIO enables same-day initiation (no oral overlap needed).

Indication: Schizophrenia in adults.

Competitive position in LAI market:

Product Manufacturer Formulation Market Position
Invega Sustenna/Trinza/Hafyera J&J/Janssen Monthly/3-monthly/6-monthly paliperidone Market leader (~51% unit share)
Abilify Maintena / Asimtufii Otsuka/Lundbeck Monthly / 2-monthly aripiprazole SOV leader (~40% in Q2'25)
ARISTADA Alkermes Monthly/6-week/2-monthly aripiprazole lauroxil ~20% SOV, stable
Uzedy Teva Monthly/bimonthly risperidone Emerging (~20% SOV)
Risperdal Consta Teva (legacy) Biweekly risperidone Declining (~10% reach)

SOV Tracker insights (from ZoomRx data Q4'23 through Q2'25):

  • ARISTADA SOV has been stable at ~20% across all quarters tracked
  • Abilify (Maintena + Asimtufii) has pulled away as SOV leader, reaching ~40% in Q2'25 driven by increased frequency
  • ARISTADA reach among Segment A (high-decile) HCPs declined in Q2'25, but SOV held steady at ~20%
  • In-person interactions dominate across all LAI brands (>80% consistently)
  • Regional variance: ARISTADA strongest in Northeast, weakest in Mid-Atlantic and South
  • ARISTADA call plan shrank from 27.4K NPIs (Q1'24) to 9.3K NPIs (Q2'25) — significant targeting contraction

Revenue trajectory:

Year Aristada Net Sales YoY Growth
2023 ~$327M (est.) —
2024 $346.2M 6%
2025 $370.0M 7%
2026E $365-385M Flat (GTN favorability won't recur)

ZoomRx engagement: ARISTADA Promotional SOV Tracker (ongoing since pre-2021, quarterly waves of 125 HCPs, 3-minute survey). Tracks reach/frequency/SOV vs Abilify Maintena/Asimtufii, Invega franchise, Uzedy/Risperdal Consta.

2.3 VIVITROL (naltrexone for extended-release injectable suspension) — STABLE CASH COW

Mechanism: Monthly IM injection of extended-release naltrexone (opioid antagonist). Blocks opioid receptors for ~30 days.

Indications: Prevention of relapse to opioid dependence after opioid detoxification; treatment of alcohol dependence.

Competitive position:

  • Only FDA-approved monthly injection that is NOT an opioid/partial agonist — differentiator for criminal justice, employers, and patients who want complete abstinence
  • AUD indication is a unique growth vector — most OUD competitors (Sublocade, Brixadi) don't have AUD data
  • Sublocade (monthly buprenorphine SC) is the primary OUD competitor — Feb 2025 label update with 12-month retention data strengthens its position
  • Brixadi (weekly/monthly buprenorphine SC) from Braeburn is emerging

Revenue trajectory:

Year Vivitrol Net Sales YoY Growth
2023 ~$400M (est.) —
2024 ~$440M (est.) ~10%
2025 ~$460M (est.) ~5%
2026E $460-480M Flat-modest

Key risk: Potential 2027 generic entry. Alkermes managing the product ahead of this event.

ZoomRx engagement: 2025 Vivitrol Promotional Effectiveness Tracking ($117,435 SOW, 1 wave, 100 interactions). Tracks VIVITROL vs SUBLOCADE and BRIXADI. Explores unaided awareness, message recall, rep quality, HCP perceptions on cost/efficacy/safety/access.

3. Competitive Threats

3.1 COBENFY (xanomeline/trospium) — BMS — THE PARADIGM SHIFT

Why it matters: Cobenfy is the first schizophrenia drug in 35 years with a genuinely new mechanism. It targets muscarinic M1/M4 receptors WITHOUT blocking D2. This means no EPS, no tardive dyskinesia, no prolactin elevation, and potentially less metabolic burden. If real-world data confirms these advantages at scale, every existing antipsychotic's positioning changes.

Approval: September 26, 2024 (FDA). Monotherapy for schizophrenia in adults.

Clinical data:

  • EMERGENT-1 & -3 (Phase 3): Statistically significant PANSS total score reduction vs placebo at 5 weeks. Effect size moderate.
  • ARISE (Phase 3, adjunctive): FAILED primary endpoint (May 2025). Cobenfy added to atypical antipsychotics did not beat placebo add-on (PANSS reduction -2.0 points, p=0.11). This was a significant setback — it eliminated the largest commercial opportunity (augmentation of existing antipsychotics).
  • Phase 4 switch study (2026): Open-label data showed symptom stability when switching from oral atypical antipsychotics to Cobenfy monotherapy over 8 weeks. No new safety signals. Provides practical switching guidance.
  • Cognitive signal: Early data suggests potential cognitive improvement — if validated, this is something NO current antipsychotic can claim.

Commercial performance:

Period Cobenfy Sales
Q1 2025 $27M
H1 2025 $62M
Q3 2025 $43M
FY 2025 ~$155M

Analyst estimates post-ARISE failure: Leerink cut 2030 estimate from $5.8B to $2.6B. Citi cut by $600M to $2.8B. The adjunctive failure meaningfully constrains Cobenfy's TAM — it cannot be layered onto existing antipsychotics, only used as monotherapy or switch.

Annual cost: ~$22,500 (significant premium over generic atypical antipsychotics)

What this means for Alkermes:

  • Lybalvi: Cobenfy competes directly for oral schizophrenia patients. But Lybalvi's weight-mitigation story is evidence-based and real-world-proven (4-year data). Cobenfy's metabolic story is theoretical (no weight gain in trials, but limited long-term data). Lybalvi also has the bipolar I indication that Cobenfy lacks. The ARISE failure limits Cobenfy to a switch/monotherapy role — it can't augment Lybalvi or any other atypical.
  • Aristada: Cobenfy is oral, not LAI. It doesn't directly compete for the adherence-challenged patient who needs an injectable. However, if Cobenfy proves durable as monotherapy, fewer patients may need the "safety net" of LAI adherence support.
  • Key watch: Cobenfy's path to bipolar disorder, Alzheimer's-related psychosis, or adjunctive re-attempt. Any label expansion changes the competitive calculus.

3.2 LAI Competitive Dynamics

Abilify Maintena/Asimtufii (Otsuka/Lundbeck): SOV leader and growing. Asimtufii (2-month formulation, approved Apr 2023) is extending Abilify's dominance. Combined reach of ~66% in Q2'25. This is ARISTADA's primary competitive threat.

Invega Sustenna/Trinza/Hafyera (J&J/Janssen): Market leader by units (~51% share). Hafyera (6-month formulation) represents the ultimate convenience play. Importantly, Alkermes' INVEGA SUSTENNA royalty expired in 2025, costing Alkermes ~$215M in manufacturing/royalty revenues — this is why FY2025 was a "transition year."

Uzedy (Teva): Risperidone LAI, approved Apr 2023. Growing from low base. Reached ~60% of HCPs in Q2'25 but only ~20% SOV. Emerging threat to ARISTADA's lower-decile HCP presence.

3.3 Oral Schizophrenia/BPD Competitors (Lybalvi's Direct Set)

Vraylar (cariprazine, AbbVie): Blockbuster. Broad label across schizophrenia, bipolar mania, bipolar depression. Strong rep presence.

Caplyta (lumateperone, Intra-Cellular): Differentiated on tolerability. Growing in bipolar depression. Positioned as the "gentle" atypical.

Rexulti (brexpiprazole, Otsuka/Lundbeck): Established in adjunctive MDD and schizophrenia. Also approved for Alzheimer's-related agitation.

4. Alkermes Corporate Context

4.1 Financial Profile

Metric FY2025
Total Revenue ~$1.5B
Proprietary Product Net Sales ~$1.2B (+9% YoY)
GAAP Net Income $241.7M
Adjusted EBITDA $394.0M
Cash & Investments $1.3B

FY2025 was a "transition year": The expiration of INVEGA SUSTENNA US royalty cost ~$215M. Proprietary product growth (Lybalvi +24%, Aristada +7%, Vivitrol ~+5%) offset this, but total revenue growth was constrained.

2026 guidance: Total revenue $1.73-1.84B. This includes ~10.5 months of LUMRYZ (narcolepsy, acquired from Avadel) — Alkermes is diversifying beyond psychiatry/addiction.

4.2 Key Alkermes Contacts (from SOWs)

  • John Fritch — VP, Data, Analytics & Insights (signed LYBALVI PET SOW)
  • Kurt Stauble — Sr. Dir., MR & CI (signed Vivitrol PET SOW)
  • Sandra Maria Rodrigues — Client contact for IQVIA TPA data access

4.3 ZoomRx Relationship

  • Master Services Agreement since July 21, 2015 (~11 years)
  • Active engagements: LYBALVI PET ($338,701), Vivitrol PET ($117,435), ARISTADA SOV Tracker (ongoing), Sales Force Tactical Optimization proposal ($89,500)
  • IQVIA TPA in place for prescriber list matching and primary market research
  • ZoomRx Senior Manager Sara Farina leads the account
  • ZoomRx CCA team (Brian Gibbs PhD, Debasish Das, Melissa Dale) positioned for analytics work

5. Key Data to Watch

Event Timeline Impact
Cobenfy real-world data / Phase 4 results Rolling 2026 Determines whether Cobenfy's tolerability advantage holds at scale
Cobenfy bipolar I disorder trial 2026-2027 Would directly compete with Lybalvi's bipolar indication
Cobenfy Alzheimer's psychosis expansion 2026-2027 Expands Cobenfy's TAM; no direct Alkermes overlap but changes BMS investment level
Lybalvi PET Q1 2025 wave (full) Delivered ~Q2 2025 First wave with Cobenfy SoV tracking — critical competitive read
Vivitrol generic entry risk 2027 Could erode Vivitrol's ~$460M revenue base
ARISTADA SOV Q2'25 report July 2025 Already delivered — Abilify pulling away at 40% SOV
Alkermes LUMRYZ integration H2 2026 Diversification execution — watch for narcolepsy cannibalization of management bandwidth
ARISE trial full publication 2026 May reveal subgroup signals or guide BMS's next adjunctive attempt

Invega Sustenna/Trinza/Hafyera (J&J/Janssen): Market leader by units (~51% share). Hafyera (6-month formulation) represents the ultimate convenience play. Importantly, Alkermes' INVEGA SUSTENNA royalty expired in 2025, costing Alkermes ~$215M in manufacturing/royalty revenues — this is why FY2025 was a "transition year."
Uzedy (Teva): Risperidone LAI, approved Apr 2023. Growing from low base. Reached ~60% of HCPs in Q2'25 but only ~20% SOV. Emerging threat to ARISTADA's lower-decile HCP presence.
3.3 Oral Schizophrenia/BPD Competitors (Lybalvi's Direct Set)
Vraylar (cariprazine, AbbVie): Blockbuster. Broad label across schizophrenia, bipolar mania, bipolar depression. Strong rep presence.
Caplyta (lumateperone, Intra-Cellular): Differentiated on tolerability. Growing in bipolar depression. Positioned as the "gentle" atypical.
Rexulti (brexpiprazole, Otsuka/Lundbeck): Established in adjunctive MDD and schizophrenia. Also approved for Alzheimer's-related agitation.

FY2025 was a "transition year": The expiration of INVEGA SUSTENNA US royalty cost ~$215M. Proprietary product growth (Lybalvi +24%, Aristada +7%, Vivitrol ~+5%) offset this, but total revenue growth was constrained.
2026 guidance: Total revenue $1.73-1.84B. This includes ~10.5 months of LUMRYZ (narcolepsy, acquired from Avadel) — Alkermes is diversifying beyond psychiatry/addiction.
